Politics.headline: 228 opened fight for democracy: KMT chairwoman
Politics.detail: The KMT chairwoman criticized the judiciary for its 'partiality' during a memorial for the 1947 uprising and government-ordered massacre in Taiwan.

Politics.headline: Chinese cyberwarfare threat serious: report
Politics.detail: A report indicates that latent malware is present in Taiwan's electronic infrastructure, which could disrupt services and agencies in the event of a conflict.

Politics.headline: Judge who discharged Arvind Kejriwal dealt with several key matters in past
Politics.detail: Details on the judge who discharged Arvind Kejriwal and the key matters they have dealt with in the past.

Politics.headline: 'Worst nightmare' for government as Greens secure first ever by-election victory
Politics.detail: The Green Party's newest MP won by a significant margin in Gorton and Denton, surpassing Labour's candidate, signaling a shift in voter preferences.

Politics.headline: Hegseth declares Anthropic a "supply chain risk to national security"
Politics.detail: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has labeled AI firm Anthropic as a supply chain risk, escalating the conflict between the Department of Defense and the AI company.

Finance & Economy.headline: US Stocks Close the Week in the Red
Finance & Economy.detail: US stocks ended the week negatively, influenced by disappointing guidance from CoreWeave and broader tech sector weakness; the S&P 500, Nasdaq, and Dow Jones all closed in the red for February.

Finance & Economy.headline: Germany Inflation Slows to 1.9% in February
Finance & Economy.detail: Germany's annual inflation rate decreased to 1.9% in February 2026, slightly below market expectations, with the harmonized index of consumer prices also edging down to 2.0%.

Finance & Economy.headline: US wholesale prices rose 0.5% in January
Finance & Economy.detail: US wholesale prices rose 0.5% in January, its biggest monthly gain in four months, beating expectations of 0.3% after a 0.4% increase in December.

Finance & Economy.headline: Sainsbury's to cut 300 jobs as it restructures tech team and Argos deliveries
Finance & Economy.detail: Sainsbury's is planning to cut 300 jobs as part of a restructuring of its tech team and Argos delivery operations.

Finance & Economy.headline: US stocks sink and oil prices rise as worries about AI, inflation and possible war hit Wall Street
Finance & Economy.detail: U.S. stocks are sinking as Wall Street gets back to hunting companies that could become losers because of the AI revolution.

Technology.headline: Geely-Backed Meizu Stops New Phone Development, Turns to AI and Auto Tech
Technology.detail: Meizu Technology Co. Ltd., controlled by Zhejiang Geely Holding Group Co. Ltd., will halt development of new self-branded smartphones for the Chinese mainland to focus on automotive software and artificial intelligence due to intensifying pressure in the smartphone market. Soaring component costs and the dominance of Huawei and Apple are accelerating consolidation and squeezing out weaker brands.

Technology.headline: Oak Ridge spawns institute to curb AI datacenter power surge
Technology.detail: Oak Ridge National Laboratory is creating an institute to link power, cooling, and workload management to ease the strain on the US grid caused by AI data centers. The lab aims to connect power, cooling, and workload management to alleviate pressure on the US power grid.

Technology.headline: Microsoft HoloLens finds second home in the military after failing battlefield tests
Technology.detail: After struggling to gain traction in battlefield testing, the Microsoft HoloLens is finding a new application within the military. The hope is that air cargo checks won't trigger the same issues encountered in previous trials.

Technology.headline: Real-Time Data Gaps Are Hindering AI Scale in Banking, Finds Info-Tech Research Group
Technology.detail: Info-Tech Research Group found that the banking industry's AI initiatives in fraud detection, personalization, and risk analytics are being held back by structural limitations in legacy data environments. Banks must process dynamic data streams and unstructured digital interactions at scale, requiring modernization of data foundations while maintaining governance and regulatory discipline.

Technology.headline: BMW CEO Oliver Zipse Wins 2026 World Car Person for Leading the Neue Klasse Project
Technology.detail: Oliver Zipse, CEO of BMW, has been named World Car Person of the Year 2026 for his leadership of the Neue Klasse project, which focuses on electric mobility, technology, and sustainability. The project aims to introduce new electric vehicles and advanced technology to BMW, with the iX3 being the first fully electric vehicle under this initiative.

Science.headline: NASA scraps its 2027 moon landing, adds two missions in 2028
Science.detail: Rather than land astronauts on the moon, the Artemis III mission will now focus on docking and space suit tests in low Earth orbit, pushing the landing to later missions.

Science.headline: Scientists find compelling evidence Earth's earliest animals were likely sea sponges
Science.detail: Scientists at MIT have found compelling chemical evidence that Earth's earliest animals were likely ancient sea sponges, based on rare molecular fingerprints in rocks over 541 million years old.

Science.headline: Stone Age boy in Sweden was buried in deerskin and a woodpecker headdress, archaeologists discover
Science.detail: Archaeologists discovered that a Stone Age boy in Sweden was buried in deerskin and a woodpecker headdress.

Science.headline: Luminescence dating confirms Roman-era gold mines in the Eastern Pyrenees
Science.detail: Luminescence dating has confirmed the existence of Roman-era gold mines in the Eastern Pyrenees, providing insights into ancient mining practices.

Science.headline: Acing this new AI exam — which its creators say is the toughest in the world — might point to the first signs of AGI
Science.detail: A new AI exam, touted as the toughest in the world, could potentially indicate the initial signs of artificial general intelligence (AGI) if an AI system manages to pass it.

Health.headline: Target says it will stop selling breakfast cereals with synthetic colors.
Health.detail: Target announced it will discontinue selling breakfast cereals containing synthetic colors.

Health.headline: Rising anger over 'lop-sided' and 'immoral' US health funding pacts with African countries.
Health.detail: Concerns are growing regarding the fairness and ethics of US health funding agreements with African nations.

Health.headline: Young people's sexual health matters. European girls aged 13-15 have world's highest rate of tobacco use for age group.
Health.detail: A report highlights the importance of sexual health for young people, noting that European girls aged 13-15 have the highest rate of tobacco use worldwide for their age group.

Health.headline: Global health in a new era of US extraction
Health.detail: A BMJ article discusses how the US is leveraging global health needs to make extractive deals with other countries, potentially undermining their sovereignty and access to resources.

Health.headline: WHO updates 2026-2027 flu vaccine composition amidst viral evolution.
Health.detail: The World Health Organization has updated the composition of the flu vaccine for 2026-2027 in response to the evolving influenza virus.

Sports.headline: Wolves secure rare win denting Villa's title bid
Sports.detail: Wolverhampton Wanderers stunned Aston Villa with a late victory, impacting Villa's title aspirations.

Sports.headline: IPL 2026 set to begin on March 28, final on May 31
Sports.detail: The Indian Premier League (IPL) 2026 is scheduled to commence on March 28 and conclude with the final match on May 31.

Sports.headline: New Zealand pacer Matt Henry granted parental leave, leaving for home after England match
Sports.detail: New Zealand's cricket team will be without Matt Henry, as he has been granted parental leave to be with his family for the arrival of his second child. Head coach Rob Walter expressed his support for Henry's departure.

Sports.headline: NFL salary cap to top $300 million per team for 2026 season
Sports.detail: The NFL salary cap is projected to exceed $300 million per team for the 2026 season, marking a significant increase.

Sports.headline: Ravi Shastri To Have Stand Named After Him At Wankhede Stadium
Sports.detail: Former India captain Ravi Shastri will have a stand named after him at Wankhede Stadium.

Entertainment.headline: Megan Thee Stallion to make her Broadway debut in "Moulin Rouge! The Musical"
Entertainment.detail: Three-time Grammy Award-winning superstar and actress Megan Thee Stallion will make her Broadway debut in the production as "Zidler", impresario of the titular nightclub, for a limited 8-week engagement.

Entertainment.headline: Pink Crashes 'The Kelly Clarkson Show' as She Eyes Time Slot Takeover
Entertainment.detail: Pink made a surprise appearance on 'The Kelly Clarkson Show', playfully hinting at potentially taking over Clarkson's time slot.

Entertainment.headline: David Bowie's daughter says she doesn't 'place blame' on parents for rehab stints
Entertainment.detail: David Bowie's daughter stated that she does not blame her parents for her rehab stints, explaining 'They were trying to help a child who was struggling in ways none of us fully understood at the time.'

Entertainment.headline: Rock and Roll Hall of Fame Announces Nominees for 2026
Entertainment.detail: The Rock and Roll Hall of Fame announced its nominees for 2026, including first-time nominees Lauren Hill, Phil Collins, Wu Tang Clan, Shakira, and Luther Vandross; Pink is the only artist to make the list in their first year of eligibility.

Entertainment.headline: Jim Carrey's New Look Sparks Speculation at César Film Awards
Entertainment.detail: Jim Carrey debuted a dramatically different appearance at the César Film Awards in Paris, sporting a clean-shaven face and a new hairstyle, which led to online speculation about possible cosmetic procedures.

Crime & Justice.headline: United States Seeks Forfeiture of Oil Tanker and 1.8M Barrels of Crude Oil That Supported Iran and Venezuela
Crime & Justice.detail: The U.S. Justice Department is seeking forfeiture of an oil tanker and 1.8 million barrels of crude oil that allegedly supported Iran and Venezuela.

Crime & Justice.headline: Florida Man Sentenced for Aggravated Identity Theft and Fraud Committed Against at Least 450 Victims
Crime & Justice.detail: A Florida man has been sentenced for aggravated identity theft and fraud impacting at least 450 victims.

Crime & Justice.headline: Former Maui Police Officer Sentenced to 65 Months for Unjustified Tasing
Crime & Justice.detail: A former Maui police officer was sentenced to 65 months in prison for unjustified tasing incidents.

Crime & Justice.headline: Waste trafficking driving toxic pollution and harming public health, says UNODC
Crime & Justice.detail: The United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (UNODC) reports that waste trafficking is driving toxic pollution and harming public health.
Crime & Justice.source: United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime

Crime & Justice.headline: Repeat offender kills 2 deputies days after $50K bond despite long violent record: police
Crime & Justice.detail: A repeat offender with a history of violence killed two Missouri deputies in a shootout after being released on a $50,000 bond.

Education.headline: Indiana lawmakers pass immigration, social media, and cellphone bills affecting schools
Education.detail: Indiana lawmakers passed bills restricting social media for minors and banning cellphones during school hours, aiming to reduce distractions and improve mental health and academic performance. The bill requires social media providers to verify user age and obtain parental consent for users under 16, limiting algorithmic content and communication for these accounts.

Education.headline: AI in Education: Bengaluru-Based Startup Attracts ₹200 Crore Funding as 50,000 Students Enroll in Smart Learning Platform
Education.detail: A Bengaluru-based startup in the education sector focusing on AI-driven smart learning has secured ₹200 Crore in funding, with 50,000 students now using the platform. This highlights the growing investment and interest in AI's role in transforming education.

Education.headline: FEATURE: Academics hail first Taigi experimental school
Education.detail: Academics are praising Taiwan's first experimental school, Beiling Primary School, which teaches courses in Hoklo (Taiwanese), and are advocating for more government funding and teacher training to expand Taigi education. The school in Kaohsiung's Lujhu District started the program in September of the previous year and has seen increased enrollment, bucking the trend of declining birthrates.

Education.headline: Saturday News Recap February 28, 2026
Education.detail: The Jamaica Information Service presents a news recap of the day's top stories. This includes updates on agriculture, budget, cabinet, commerce, culture, climate change, development, economic growth, education, energy, environment, finance, foreign affairs, health, and other areas.

Education.headline: House education bills that passed
Education.detail: A massive deregulation bill repealing 40 provisions in education and higher education and making changes to 15 more topics, including powers of the governing bodies of school corporations and public-private agreements by charter schools for the construction or renovation of schools. Removes transportation requirements in the approval criteria for the school-age child care project fund.

Lifestyle.headline: The quarter-zip is the breakout star of 2026 – and I think I know why
Lifestyle.detail: Jess Cartner-Morley discusses the rising popularity of the quarter-zip garment in fashion, exploring its comfort and style aspects.

Lifestyle.headline: 'Watching my six year old deadlift 35kg was pretty cool': meet the children who work out
Lifestyle.detail: An article in The Guardian profiles children who engage in weightlifting and strength training, highlighting the experiences and perspectives of these young athletes.

Lifestyle.headline: Family of 6 exposes 'insanely expensive' airport food with pizza order, igniting online backlash
Lifestyle.detail: A family of six's experience with high airport food prices, specifically a pizza order, has sparked debate and criticism online.

Lifestyle.headline: Bhagyashree shares recipe for 'magic water' made with simple kitchen ingredients 'that can transform your body'
Lifestyle.detail: Bhagyashree shares a recipe for a 'magic water' using simple kitchen ingredients, claiming it can transform the body.

Lifestyle.headline: Woman doubts getting a job that BF threatened to break up over: Reddit reacts
Lifestyle.detail: A woman's dilemma about accepting a job offer due to her boyfriend's threat to end the relationship is discussed, with Reddit users offering their perspectives.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Confronting an uncomfortable past
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Today marks the 79th anniversary of the 228 Incident, an anti-government protest that began on Taipei's Dihua Street after a Taiwanese widow was assaulted by Monopoly Bureau agents, sparking public outrage over official abuse.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL — Keeping the health pork barrel
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Despite calls from medical organizations and a corruption scandal, lawmakers retained the health pork barrel in the 2026 General Appropriations Act, with President Marcos' approval, allocating billions to programs where politicians can claim credit for state-funded aid.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Trump's Board of Peace meeting raises more questions than answers
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Board of Peace meeting was long on showmanship and short on substance, with concerns raised about the exclusion of the West Bank from settlement plans and the lack of clarity regarding the mandate of the International Security Force.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Hyundai's 9 Trillion Won Bid to End Saemangeum's 35-Year Hope Torment
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Hyundai Motor Group plans to invest 9 trillion Korean won in Saemangeum to build an AI data center, robot manufacturing plant, and energy infrastructure, aiming to establish a 'Physical AI ecosystem,' but requires significant government support to overcome regulatory and infrastructural hurdles.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Alberta manages growth while B.C. hikes spending and taxes
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The two provinces delivered very different budgets.

World.headline: China to accelerate lunar mission plans this year
World.detail: China plans to accelerate its lunar mission, focusing on research and technology development related to lunar exploration in the coming year.

World.headline: At least 19 killed, 26 injured following Afghanistan-Pakistan border clashes
World.detail: Deadly clashes at the Afghanistan-Pakistan border have resulted in multiple fatalities and injuries, escalating tensions between the two nations.

World.headline: Trump says he is 'not happy' with nuclear talks, no final decision on Iran strike
World.detail: Former U.S. President Donald Trump expressed dissatisfaction with the ongoing nuclear talks with Iran, while also stating that no final decision has been made regarding a potential strike against the country.

World.headline: Tram derails in Milan, 1 killed, dozens injured: local media
World.detail: A tram in Milan has derailed, causing one death and injuries to dozens of people, according to local media reports, with investigations underway to determine the cause.

World.headline: Omani FM says Iran agreed to zero enriched uranium stockpiling in talks 'breakthrough'
World.detail: Oman's Foreign Minister announced a 'breakthrough' in talks with Iran, stating that Iran has agreed to have zero enriched uranium stockpiles.

Zimbabwe.headline: Constitution Amendment: Zimbabwe Churches Objects to Mnangagwa's Power-Grab
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe's leading Christian bodies have issued a strongly worded pastoral statement opposing the proposed Constitution of Zimbabwe Amendment (No. 3), objecting to Mnangagwa's Power-Grab.

Zimbabwe.headline: New ZiG banknote series circulation to start April 7
Zimbabwe.detail: The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe (RBZ) will introduce a new series of ZiG banknotes on April 7, 2026, Governor Dr John Mushayavanhu has announced.

Zimbabwe.headline: Don't be misled into extending stay in office, churches warns ED
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwean churches are warning President ED not to be misled into extending his stay in office.

Zimbabwe.headline: RBZ introduces new ZiG notes, adds higher denominations
Zimbabwe.detail: The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe is introducing new ZiG notes and adding higher denominations.

Zimbabwe.headline: Minister Soda urges media to keep public informed to drive Vision 2030
Zimbabwe.detail: Minister Soda has urged the media in Zimbabwe to keep the public informed in order to drive Vision 2030.
